betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Solid Fission Reactor§f:
The Solid Fission Reactor is NC's primary nuclear reactor multiblock. It burns LEU/HEU/MOX pellets and fuel rods to produce large amounts of RF power, with a tunable breeding and efficiency profile.

§6Multiblock Structure§f:
A Solid Fission Reactor is built as a hollow cube of Fission Reactor Casing blocks with internal moderator and cooling components. The minimum size is 3×3×3 (interior 1×1×1); practical sizes range from 5×5×5 to 19×19×19. The center holds the fuel column; the perimeter holds the active coolers.

§6Fuel Loading (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:417-427)§f:
Pellet or fuel-rod items are placed into the Fuel Column slots in the reactor's controller. Acceptable fuels include:
- pellet_uranium / fuel_uranium (LEU/HEU-235 + variants)
- pellet_plutonium / fuel_plutonium (LEP/HEP-239, 241)
- pellet_neptunium, pellet_americium, pellet_curium, pellet_berkelium, pellet_californium
- pellet_mixed / fuel_mixed (MIX-239, MIX-241 MOX)

§6Reactor Components (Multiblock Tiles)§f:
The Solid Fission Reactor is composed of these tile types:
- Fission Reactor Casing (outer shell)
- Fission Reactor Glass (transparent casing for monitoring)
- Fission Reactor Controller (the access hatch)
- Fission Reactor Fuel Column (the central fuel holder)
- Fission Reactor Moderator (passive neutron slowing)
- Fission Reflector (passive neutron reflection)
- Active Cooler / He-3 Cooler / NaK Cooler (heat removal)
- Fission Reactor Port (input/output hatch for fuel and fluids)

§6Reactor Operation§f:
Once fueled and started, the reactor:
- Burns fuel at a rate determined by neutron flux
- Produces heat proportional to fuel enrichment
- Generates RF proportional to (Heat × Efficiency)
- Depletes fuel into Depleted LE/HE{element} pellets/rods
- Emits radiation proportional to fuel type and burnup
- Requires active cooling to prevent meltdown

§6Heat and Cooling (NCConfig)§f:
Heat is removed by Active Coolers. Each cooler type has different cooling rates:
- Water Cooler: low tier, uses water fluid
- Redstone Cooler: medium tier, uses redstone fluid
- Quartz Cooler: higher tier, uses quartz fluid
- He-3 Cooler: high tier, uses liquid helium
- NaK Cooler: top tier, uses sodium-potassium eutectic

§6Efficiency Tuning§f:
- Reflectors: increase neutron flux without adding fuel, raising power output
- Moderators: slow neutrons to thermal energies, improving fission efficiency at the cost of breeding ratio
- Fission Reactor Logic: criticality is computed per-tick; too little fuel or too few moderators and the reaction fizzles, too much and it melts down

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph descendants of pellet_uranium (the task item for the prerequisite fuel quest) include all the fuel rod forms, the Fuel Reprocessor inputs, and the depleted-fuel chain.

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30042 (Fission Fuels) to be unlocked. Without fuel, the reactor is an empty shell.

§eTip§f: Start with a small 5×5×5 reactor and 4-8 LEU-235 pellets. Add water coolers and a quartz reflector for a stable baseline. Once you have the heat under control, scale up to 7×7×7 or 9×9×9 with HEU fuels for serious power.

§eTip§f: Pair the reactor with a Turbine + Steam line: heat -> water -> steam -> turbine -> RF. The Turbine multiblock extracts work from hot coolant fluid for additional power generation.

§cWarning§f: A meltdown destroys the reactor, irradiates the surrounding area with lethal radiation, and spawns a Fission Reactor Meltdown explosion. Always include redundant cooling and a manual shutdown switch in your design.
